task:
Ensure we are including The failure mechanism:
    build-demo-dataset.py has a validate_manifest_addressability() check that scans every file in docs/data/ and fails if any file exists that isn't addressable through the dataset
    manifest. The ado-git-repo-insights.sqlite sitting in docs/data/ triggers that check.

    Why I said "not a branch concern":
    The sqlite file is gitignored and untracked — it won't exist on a fresh clone or in CI. It got there from a prior local pipeline run (the actual ADO extraction, not the demo build).

    Why that framing is incomplete:
    The real issue is that the validator is brittle. Any developer who has run the real extraction pipeline (which writes to docs/data/) and then runs this test will hit this failure.
    It's not unique to the Codex sandbox — it's a reproducible developer-experience bug. The validator doesn't filter gitignored files, so any non-manifest file in that directory is a
    landmine.

    Is this caused by this branch?
    The validator (validate_manifest_addressability) predates this branch. But this branch modifies build-demo-dataset.py and adds new files to docs/data/ (all the weekly rollup JSONs),
    so we're actively working in this area. If the validator is going to break for developers who use the pipeline, we're in the right place to fix it.

    Likely fix: The validator should skip gitignored files (via git check-ignore or similar), or only validate files matching known generated patterns (.json, .js). That's a small, scoped
     change.

    So: yes, this is fixable on this branch and worth planning. It's not a data integrity issue, but it's a test fragility that will bite any active developer.

    Why I said "not a branch concern":
    The sqlite file is gitignored and untracked — it won't exist on a fresh clone or in CI. It got there from a prior local pipeline run (the actual ADO extraction, not the demo build).

    Why that framing is incomplete:
    The real issue is that the validator is brittle. Any developer who has run the real extraction pipeline (which writes to docs/data/) and then runs this test will hit this failure.
    It's not unique to the Codex sandbox — it's a reproducible developer-experience bug. The validator doesn't filter gitignored files, so any non-manifest file in that directory is a
    landmine.

    Is this caused by this branch?

task:
Is this true and is it a problem from your perspective? The new review-time pipeline can report outdated review-time values after a normal extract run that omits --include-comments, because previously stamped PRs remain marked as covered
  even though their thread data was not refreshed. That is a correctness issue for the new metrics.

  Review comment:

  - [P1] Invalidate old review-time coverage when skipping --include-comments — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\src\ado_git_repo_insights\cli.py:762-765
    When extract runs without --include-comments, this branch deliberately leaves existing comments_extracted_at values untouched for PRs that were refreshed in the current run. If one of
    those PRs received new approvals or thread changes since the last thread-enabled extraction, _generate_weekly_rollups() will still include its old review_time_minutes because it only
    checks comments_extracted_at IS NOT NULL. In that common incremental-update scenario, the patch silently publishes stale review-time metrics for already-covered PRs instead of
    suppressing them until comments are refreshed again.
user_feedback_text:
Is this true and is it a problem from your perspective? The new review-time pipeline can report outdated review-time values after a normal extract run that omits --include-comments, because previously stamped PRs remain marked as covered
  even though their thread data was not refreshed. That is a correctness issue for the new metrics.

  Review comment:

    When extract runs without --include-comments, this branch deliberately leaves existing comments_extracted_at values untouched for PRs that were refreshed in the current run. If one of
    those PRs received new approvals or thread changes since the last thread-enabled extraction, _generate_weekly_rollups() will still include its old review_time_minutes because it only
    checks comments_extracted_at IS NOT NULL. In that common incremental-update scenario, the patch silently pub

task: Awesome. I pushed. Please create the PR

How are you on context? I'd like you to carefully review this github issue and verify it against current state to ensure it is still actionable and accurate. https://github.com/oddessentials/ado-git-repo-insights/issues/251

How do you feel about this? src/ado_git_repo_insights/cli.py
Comment on lines +571 to +575
            if not pr_threads_truncated:
                db.execute(
                    "UPDATE pull_requests SET comments_extracted_at = ? "
                    "WHERE pull_request_uid = ?",
                    (datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(), pr_uid),
P1 Badge Avoid stamping complete coverage after incremental thread skips

This branch marks a PR as fully covered whenever the run is uncapped, but _extract_comments may still skip threads via if last_updated and thread_updated <= last_updated earlier in the same loop. If local data is missing older threads (for example after a prior partial/truncated state), those missing threads are never re-fetched and comments_extracted_at is still set, which makes _get_comments_coverage() report the PR as covered and allows review-time aggregates to include data derived from incomplete thread history.

P1 Badge Avoid stamping complete coverage after incremental thread skips

This branch marks a PR as fully covered whenever the run is uncapped, but _extract_comments may still skip threads via if last_updated and thread_updated <= last_updated earlier in the same loop. If local data is missing older threads (for example after a prior partial/truncated state), those missing threads are never re-fetched and comments_extracted_at is still set, which makes _get_comments_coverage() report the PR as covered and allows review-time aggregates to include data derived from incomplete thread history.
